title: pouteria cuspidata subsp. robusta
intro: Pouteria cuspidata subsp. robusta, also known as the robust sapote, is a tropical fruit-bearing tree species belonging to the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Central America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The robust sapote has been reported to have several health benefits, including antioxidant, antidiabetic, and antiparasitic activities, which can help protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the tree's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
